trigger: Cross-repository HGI-401 preparation found current routers and intent owners still describing accepted HGI-206 as in progress with grading pending.
claims: HGI-206 is accepted and published at semantic commit c2f41c10dca141c7cb7e066b3a2bff48048101fc with validation closeout 8c983d992f0b5eca46eb2777debded7dbccf0e7a.

The repository-local SPEC is implemented, its execution plan belongs in completed history, no execution plan is currently active, and current harness verification routes to the validation receipt rather than the pre-acceptance candidate alone.
The lifecycle correction changes routing and effectiveness disposition only; it does not rewrite the accepted epoch evidence or product implementation.

impactLedger.owner: TaxKit execution-plan lifecycle indexes and retained completed plan
impactLedger.evidence: All repository-local HGI-200 through HGI-208 tasks have accepted receipts; the plan no longer owns active work.
impactLedger.nonClaim: Moving the plan does not complete HGI-400 or any external operation.

impactLedger.evidence: HGI-206 validation records local acceptance and Git publication; the SPEC becomes implemented/supporting.
impactLedger.nonClaim: Implemented documentation does not establish release, registry, provider, deployment, public-site, or consumer actuality.

impactLedger.owner: TaxKit documentation router and dated evidence index
impactLedger.evidence: Current navigation now routes completed requalification to HGI-206-validation and keeps the candidate as retained pre-acceptance evidence.
impactLedger.nonClaim: A router link is not independent proof.

impactLedger.evidence: The accepted result is recorded while the stale lifecycle route is explicitly revised; the no-comparison result remains inconclusive.
impactLedger.nonClaim: The correction proves no causal effect, variance stability, future epoch, or cross-repository qualification.

impactLedger.owner: Existing public and package entry points
impactLedger.evidence: No setup, export, consumer contract, app behavior, or package ownership changed.
impactLedger.nonClaim: Preservation is not a new public contract claim.

impactLedger.surface: skills, agent instructions, lint, config, tests and CI
impactLedger.owner: Existing TaxKit policy, skill, tooling and workflow owners
impactLedger.evidence: The correction changes only current documentation lifecycle and is exercised by existing documentation/skill/repository gates.
impactLedger.nonClaim: Passing local gates does not prove hosted CI.

impactLedger.evidence: No operation, principal, resource, environment, recovery path, or authority boundary changed.

impactLedger.surface: product code, schemas, APIs, generated docs and public MDX
impactLedger.evidence: The defect is a completed-task lifecycle contradiction only; no product or generated contract changed.
impactLedger.nonClaim: No product behavior, generated output, or public content is established.

impactLedger.surface: release, registry, provider, deployment and external consumers
impactLedger.evidence: This correction performs no external readback or consequence beyond the later ordinary Git publication of repository documentation.
impactLedger.nonClaim: No version, tag, hosted release, registry publication, deployment, provider, public-site, recovery, or external-consumer outcome is established.

retainedFailures.command: bun run test:skills && bun run check:docs && bun run check:runbooks && bun run check:repository-paths && git diff --check
retainedFailures.failure: The pre-stage documentation inventory still included the deleted tracked active-plan path and reported five missing-frontmatter violations.
retainedFailures.recovery: Stage the explicit plan move and candidate-owned documentation paths so the Git-index-backed policy observes the intended lifecycle tree, then rerun the same checks.
retainedFailures.nonClaim: The failure is not a passing docs receipt and does not establish a semantic or provider defect.

rollback: Revert the lifecycle-correction validation commit and semantic commit in reverse order; preserve the original HGI-206 candidate, validation, failed orchestration, results and receipts.
limitations: This candidate is not accepted until the named checks pass against the committed semantic tree and the validation receipt is published.
The central HGI-400 target must be re-pinned after the accepted correction; the old frozen TaxKit target cannot be used as current-truth qualification.
nonClaims: No HGI-400 worker or grader has run.
No provider, deployment, release, registry, credential, public-site, recovery, or external-consumer state was read or changed.
